What is serialism?

600.q: What is the Rite of Spring and why was it controversial?
600.a: Stravinsky's 1913 ballet — its premiere caused a riot due to dissonant music and primitive themes

600.q: What is musique concrète?
600.a: Music made from recorded real-world sounds — pioneered by Pierre Schaeffer in the 1940s

600.q: Who was John Coltrane?
600.a: Legendary jazz saxophonist known for A Love Supreme — pushed the boundaries of improvisation

600.q: What is twelve-tone technique?
600.a: Schoenberg's method of composing with all 12 chromatic scale notes treated equally

600.q: What is a Castrato?
600.a: A male singer castrated before puberty to preserve a high voice — common in Baroque opera

600.q: Difference between a symphony and a concerto?
600.a: Symphony: for full orchestra. Concerto: features a solo instrument accompanied by orchestra

600.q: What is the Hungarian Rhapsody?
600.a: Piano compositions by Franz Liszt based on Hungarian folk themes — No. 2 is most famous

600.q: Who is Miles Davis and why is he significant?
600.a: Jazz trumpeter who led multiple revolutions — from bebop to modal jazz to fusion

600.q: Beethoven's most remarkable symphony achievement?
600.a: The 9th Symphony — composed when Beethoven was completely deaf

600.q: What is counterpoint?
600.a: The art of combining two or more independent melodic lines — Bach was the master

600.q: Baroque vs Classical vs Romantic periods?
600.a: Baroque (1600–1750): ornate, polyphonic. Classical (1750–1820): balanced. Romantic (1820–1900): emotional

600.q: What is the significance of the 432Hz vs 440Hz debate?
600.a: 440Hz is standard concert pitch; 432Hz proponents claim it sounds more natural — no scientific basis

600.q: What is Impressionist music?
600.a: Late 19th century style evoking mood through colour and texture — Debussy and Ravel are key composers

600.q: What is a fugue?
600.a: A contrapuntal composition in which a subject is introduced by one voice and imitated by others

600.q: What is the Gamelan?
600.a: A traditional ensemble music of Java and Bali — uses metallophones, xylophones, and gongs
